data.title: 17LIVE Group Buys Back 18,600 Shares
data.description:
17LIVE Group repurchased 18,600 shares at between SG$0.83 and SG$0.86 each, totaling SG$15,767, according to a Friday filing on the Singapore Exchange.

The social entertainment company has now bought back 415,700 shares, representing 0.23% of its issued shares, excluding treasury shares, under the current buyback mandate.

data.title: Orica Could Unveil A$500 Million Buyback at Investor Day — Market Talk
data.description: Wednesday could be a good day for Orica investors betting on additional capital management. Barrenjoey believes Orica could announce an on-market share buyback program worth around A$500 million during its investor day, pointing to the headroom to its 30-40% gearing target. Analyst Brook Campbell-Crawford says the investor day could also offer more color on medium-term financial targets for Orica's Digital division. It thinks this would be well received by investors. "We have an overweight rating on Orica given its strong growth outlook, prospect for capital returns, and attractive valuation of 16x near-term EPS (versus its 5-year average of 18x)," Barrenjoey says. (david.winning@wsj.com; @dwinningWSJ)

data.title: New Zealand Stocks Inch Higher to Begin the Week
data.description: The NZX 50 added 13 points or 0.1% higher to 12,414 in Monday morning session after ending in the red during the previous session, supported by gains mainly in financials, producer manufacturing, and energy minerals.

data.title: Insignia Unlikely to Catch a Significantly Higher Bid — Market Talk
data.description: Any further bids for Insignia Financial are unlikely to represent much of an advance on the A$5.00/share proposed by its current private-equity suitors, Barrenjoey analysts write in a note. Analysts Andrew Adams and Niva Chandrasekaran tell clients that there is scope for higher bids but any increase is likely to come at a much smaller increment than those seen in the process so far. They highlight the Australian wealth manager's acknowledgement that it plans to recommend an acquisition at A$5.00/share, in the absence of any superior proposal. Barrenjoey lowers its recommendation to neutral from overweight and keeps a A$4.95 target price, which includes a 10% takeover premium. Shares are at A$4.68 ahead of the open. (stuart.condie@wsj.com)

data.title: Macquarie picks Aussie defensive industrial stocks amid subdued demand
data.description:
** Analysts at Macquarie say the Australia-New Zealand Industrials sectors still show signs of pretty muted demand

** However, says businesses that have trimmed costs, improved balance are readied for recovery

** Brokerage chooses defensive stocks - Amcor , Brambles , Cleanaway Waste and James Hardie as its top picks from the local sector

** Industrial universe remains very focused on cost reduction and efficiency, especially as market conditions remain tepid - Macquarie

** Says Amcor has a solid defensive exposure at an attractive valuation and that Berry integration is the key tenet of brokerage's thesis

** Macquarie says valuation of Brambles remains attractive given its strong return profile and a "clear intent" to lift returns

** Brokerage says Cleanaway's new business continues to increasingly support its outlook

** Adds James Hardie can manage any tariff impacts and is flexing its cost base with good effect

data.title: nIB Holdings to be Removed From ASX 100 Index
data.description: nIB Holdings will be removed from the S&P/ASX 100 index before the opening of trading on March 24, according to a March 7 Australian bourse filing.

data.title: Tartana Agrees With Investor to Lapse 500,000 Convertible Notes
data.description:
Tartana Minerals (ASX: TAT) agreed with an investor to lapse 500,000 convertible notes, due for redemption on March 5, without conversion, according to a March 7 filing with the Australian bourse.

The AU$500,000 principal will be converted into an unsecured cash advance, bearing 15% annual interest, paid monthly, the filing said.

The company will seek shareholder approval at its next general meeting to issue new convertible notes for the outstanding balance, also at 15% interest, convertible into equity at AU$0.10 per share with a 12-month maturity, the filing added.

If approval is not granted, the investor may demand repayment in cash, per the filing.

data.title: Dasin Retail Trustee Manager's Major Shareholder Files Joinder Request in Hong Kong Arbitration
data.description:
Dasin Retail Trust said New Harvest Investments, which holds 70% of the trustee-manager, filed a request to join Dasin Retail Trust Management as a respondent in an ongoing Hong Kong arbitration against Zhang Zhencheng, Shun Fung Investment, and Aqua Wealth, according to a Friday filing on the Singapore Exchange.

The arbitration stems from alleged breaches of a 2021 sale and purchase agreement and a shareholder agreement.

Singapore's High Court granted a six-month moratorium in January, later allowing New Harvest's joinder request in February.

data.title: Southern Cross Teams Up With Australian Football League
data.description:
Southern Cross Media Group has secured a three-year partnership with the Australian Football League (AFL), according to a March 7 filing with the Australian bourse.

Under the agreement, the company's LiSTNR and the Triple M network's radio stations will deliver more than 850 hours of live AFL match coverage per season, along with increased editorial support in key AFL markets in Sydney, Brisbane, and the Gold Coast, the filing said.

Financial terms were not disclosed.

data.title: Malaysia Smelting to Issue 420 Million Bonus Shares
data.description:
Malaysia Smelting Berhad plans to issue 420 million bonus shares on a 1-for-1 basis, according to a Friday filing on the Singapore Exchange.

The proposal would double the company's issued share capital to 840 million shares without capitalizing reserves or increasing share capital value.

The theoretical ex-bonus price is estimated at 1.1274 ringgit based on the five-day volume-weighted average price of 2.2548 ringgit.

data.title: Canada's Liberal Party elect Mark Carney as new leader
data.description:
Bank of Canada Governor Bank of England Governor Mark Carney is the new Prime Minister of Canada.

Carney clocked 86% of the votes!

Beat former Finance Minister Chrystia Freeland who came in second place.

Canada's next federal election is scheduled for October 20, 2025. It can be called on an earlier date if the government loses a confidence vote in the House of Commons or if the Prime Minister requests the Governor General dissolve Parliament. Carney has indicated that he may call an early federal election, potentially by the end of April or early May 2025

---

Justin Trudeau has been the Canadian prime minister since 2015

  • leader of the Liberal Party since 2013

data.title: Stocks Poised for Lower Open — Barrons.com
data.description:
By Janet H. Cho

Stock market futures were falling ahead of economic data that will further illustrate the impact of Trump administration tariffs and policies on consumer spending, inflation, and sentiment.

At 6:11 p.m. Eastern time on Sunday, the Dow Jones Industrial Average futures were down 302 points, or 0.7%, the S&P 500 futures were down 0.8%, and Nasdaq Composite futures were down nearly 1%.

The Trump administration's tariffs have weighed on stocks, and there are signs of more volatility to come. A broad set of 25% tariffs on steel and aluminum imports is set to begin on Wednesday.

After the White House imposed 25% tariffs on goods from Canada and Mexico and then delayed them until April, stocks weakened. The Dow Jones Industrial Average closed down 2.37% last week and is down two of the past three weeks, according to Dow Jones Market Data. It is up 0.61% so far this year.

The S&P 500 index closed down 3.1% last week, its largest one-week percentage decline since the week ended Sept. 6, 2024, according to Dow Jones Market Data. It is down for three straight weeks and down 1.89% so far this year.

The tech-heavy Nasdaq Composite closed down 3.45% last week. It is down 9.14% over the past three weeks, its largest three-week percentage decline since the week ending Sept. 30, 2022. The Nasdaq is down 5.77% this year.

This week's economic highlights include the small business optimism index reading for February from the National Federation of Independent Business and the release of the February consumer price index on Wednesday. The University of Michigan will report its preliminary Consumer Sentiment Index for March on Friday.

Write to Janet H. Cho at janet.cho@dowjones.com

This content was created by Barron's, which is operated by Dow Jones & Co. Barron's is published independently from Dow Jones Newswires and The Wall Street Journal.

data.title: GQG Partners' Funds Under Management Little Changed at $160.5 Billion in February
data.description:
GQG Partners reported total funds under management (FUM) of $160.5 billion, compared with $160.4 billion in January, according to a Monday filing with the Australian bourse.

International equity FUM rose to $61.9 billion from $60.6 billion, and global equity FUM grew to $41.6 billion from $41.3 billion. Emerging markets equity fell to $37.6 billion from $40 billion, while US equity FUM rose to $19.4 billion from $18.5 billion.

Net flows stood at $1.1 billion for the month, bringing the year-to-date total to $2.8 billion.

data.title: Global Equities Roundup: Market Talk
data.description:
The latest Market Talks covering Equities. Published exclusively on Dow Jones Newswires throughout the day.

1738 ET - Fonterra Shareholders' Fund rises 5% after the dairy cooperative upgrades its annual earnings view, putting the stock within sight of a new seven-year high. Fonterra now expects EPS of NZ$0.55-NZ$0.75 in FY 2025. That's up from prior EPS guidance of NZ$0.40-NZ$0.60. CEO Miles Hurrell says the upgrade reflects the underlying strength of Fonterra's core Ingredients business. It also signals resilience in the Consumer business that Fonterra wants to sell, raising investors' hopes that it will attract a favorable price from prospective buyers. Fonterra is at NZ$5.45 today, moving within sight of NZ$5.59 that represents its peak over the past 12 months and which was its highest level since January 2018. (david.winning@wsj.com; @dwinningWSJ)

1829 ET - The risk of new delays to Deep Yellow's Tumas project helps to keep Jefferies at underperform on the aspiring uranium producer. Deep Yellow aims to make a final investment decision on Tumas this month, while arranging a debt package and finding offtake partners. Current pre-production capex is estimated at US$360 million, plus US$51 million in pre-production capitalized operating costs, and US$30 million in working capital. "We see risk of elevated capex and project delays to the 2026 first production timeline," says analyst Daniel Roden. "Deep Yellow has also stated the development of Tumas will only be pursued with an unquantified supportive uranium price to provide the best shareholder returns." (david.winning@wsj.com; @dwinningWSJ)

1821 ET - TPG Telecom's move to raise prices for some of its postpaid mobile customers is unlikely to have an impact on the Australian company's full-year outlook, Barrenjoey analyst Eric Choi says. He writes in a note to clients that TPG appears to be addressing the 20% of its Vodafone Australia back-book that it missed when raising prices last year. He thinks the oversight was likely due to TPG being in the midst of simplifying its plans. The latest move could add about A$15 million in 2025 revenue but is probably already captured by existing guidance, Choi adds. Barrenjoey has a neutral rating and A$5.25 target price on the stock, which is at A$4.47 ahead of Monday's session. (stuart.condie@wsj.com)

1811 ET - Uranium prices may have fallen 12% so far this year but Jefferies believes the medium-term outlook for the nuclear fuel remains bright. It attributes recent weakness in uranium prices to heightened geopolitical and macro uncertainties, cautious utility buying, and increased confidence in supply. "The risk-off environment has largely kept buyers out of the spot market, while U.S. utilities continue to engage in the term market, with 11 term awards totalling 15.3 million lbs already secured in 2025," analyst Daniel Roden says. That has driven a widening contango between term prices of uranium at US$80.00/lb and spot prices of US$63.95/lb. Still, Jefferies stays constructive on the medium-term outlook, supported by disciplined production strategies, supply deficits, and rising global reactor demand from China, U.S. and Europe. (david.winning@wsj.com; @dwinningWSJ)

1754 ET - Australia's S&P/ASX 200 looks set to bounce after finishing last week at its lowest closing level since August. ASX futures are up by 0.9%, suggesting the benchmark index should recover some of Friday's 1.8% fall. The ASX 200's worst day since September wrapped up a third consecutive weekly fall. With little economic data due this week and Australia's central bank not set to announce its next interest rate decision until next month, investors may remain focused on global geopolitical and trade worries. Major U.S. indices pared their recent losses on Friday, although they still finished lower for the week. The DJIA added 0.5%, the S&P 500 gained 0.6%, and the Nasdaq Composite put on 0.7%. (stuart.condie@wsj.com)

data.title: Fonterra Shareholders' Fund Jumps 5% on Earnings Guidance Upgrade — Market Talk
data.description: Fonterra Shareholders' Fund rises 5% after the dairy cooperative upgrades its annual earnings view, putting the stock within sight of a new seven-year high. Fonterra now expects EPS of NZ$0.55-NZ$0.75 in FY 2025. That's up from prior EPS guidance of NZ$0.40-NZ$0.60. CEO Miles Hurrell says the upgrade reflects the underlying strength of Fonterra's core Ingredients business. It also signals resilience in the Consumer business that Fonterra wants to sell, raising investors' hopes that it will attract a favorable price from prospective buyers. Fonterra is at NZ$5.45 today, moving within sight of NZ$5.59 that represents its peak over the past 12 months and which was its highest level since January 2018. (david.winning@wsj.com; @dwinningWSJ)

data.title: BPH Global Reports Gold, Copper Assays From First Phase of Seaweed Cultivation
data.description:
BPH Global Ltd said assay results from the first phase of the cultivation of the sesuvium portucalastrum seaweed in the laboratory in clean, filtered seawater from the Singapore Island of Sentosa showed gold assays of up to 14.9 milligram-per-kilogram (mg/kg) and copper assays of up to 10.9 mg/kg, according to a March 7 Australian bourse filing.

The cultivation was conducted at Gaia Mariculture's Singapore facility, and initial tests revealed "exceptional" silver and cobalt levels, the filing said.

The second phase will shift focus to cultivating the seaweed species in the waters of Johor, Malaysia, which are close to shipping lanes and industrial activity, allowing it to test its hypothesis that seaweed absorbs higher mineral concentrations in dirty polluted waters than seaweed grown in pristine waters.

data.title: Food Empire Donates SG$200,000 to National University of Singapore for Food Science Bursary
data.description:
Food Empire Holdings donated SG$200,000 to the National University of Singapore (NUS) to establish the Food Empire Food Science & Technology Bursary, according to a Friday filing on the Singapore Exchange.

The move aims to support undergraduates in the Food Science and Technology program.

data.title: BHP earns right to take 75% stake in Cobre's Botswana projects
data.description:
Mining conglomerate BHP has taken a right to acquire a 75% stake in Australia's Cobre's Kitlanya projects in Botswana in exchange for $25 million in funding for exploration activities, the copper explorer said on Monday.

The deal follows Cobre's selection in BHP's Xplor program in January last year in which the smaller rival received $500,000 to accelerate exploration plans in its Kalahari copper projects in the African nation.

The agreement sees at least $5 million of committed funding to be paid to Cobre within two years of its commencement date, with a planned budget of $7 million for exploration expenditure starting next month.

"The partnership with BHP will provide us with the funding and support necessary to implement a technology-driven work programme designed to discover the Tier 1 deposits we believe may be hosted in our Kitlanya East and West projects," Cobre Chief Executive Adam Woolridge said.

data.title: ValueMax Issues 748,822 Shares Following Warrant Exercise
data.description:
ValueMax Group said its issued share count increased to 869.2 million after allotting 748,822 shares at SG$0.36 each through warrant exercise on Friday, according to a same-day filing on the Singapore Stock Exchange.

The new shares will trade on the Singapore Exchange on March 11. The company has 78.6 million warrants outstanding, expiring on Sept. 14, 2026.

data.title: ASX Preview: Australian Shares Set for Higher Open Amid US Fed Chair's Reassurance on US Economy
data.description:
Australian equities are poised to open higher on Monday after Wall Street rose Friday as US Federal Reserve Chair Jerome Powell eased investors' worries over economic uncertainties.

Powell commented on the macroeconomic environment saying that the US "continues to be in a good place" and the labor market is "solid," adding that the Fed will wait to see the effects of the Trump administration's decisions on trade, immigration, fiscal policy, and regulation.

The Nasdaq Composite gained 0.7%, the S&P 500 rose 0.6%, and the Dow Jones Industrial Average ended 0.5% higher.

In corporate news, Star Entertainment Group will be removed from the ASX 200 index before the opening of trading on March 24 as a result of S&P Down Jones Indices' quarterly review.

Platinum Investment Management reported total funds under management of AU$10.76 billion as of Feb. 28, down from AU$10.99 billion as of Jan. 31.

Australia's benchmark index fell 1.8% Friday to close at 7,948.20.

data.title: Macquarie lifts its PT for Australia's Orica on stronger outlook
data.description:
** Macquarie hikes its Price Target (PT) for shares of Orica to A$20.91 from A$20.78, 'Outperform' on stock

** Brokerage accounts for its 3% FY25 earnings outlook lift for the mining solutions provider, reflecting its stronger Blasting business

** Also sees potential buy back announcement on March 12

** 11 of 13 analysts rate the stock "buy" or higher, 2 "hold"; their median PT is A$20.90 – according to data compiled by LSEG

** Stock up 0.5% at A$17.03 as of last close

($1 = 1.5876 Australian dollars)

data.title: MS lifts PT on Synlait Milk on quicker-than-estimated turnaround, shares rise
data.description:
** Shares of New Zealand's Synlait Milk gain 2.2% to NZ$0.92

** Analysts at Bell Potter raise PT on the infant formula maker's Australia shares to A$0.90 from A$0.425; keep stock rating at "hold"

** Brokerage says turnaround at Synlait Milk has been faster than it had expected

** However, Bell Potter remains cognisant of the potential of peer A2 Milk to vertically integrate into supply chain assets through 2025

** Says A2's impact would likely be detrimental to Synlait's advanced nutrition business in coming years

** A2 Milk shares down 0.5%

** Synlait up over 100% YTD

data.title: Here's a EUR/USD forecast to 1.20 after the German fiscal bazooka lst week
data.description:
Catching up on some of the revised forecasts after Germany ditched its debt brake, ICYMI:

  • Germany will set up €500 billion infrastructure fund and a special defense fund
  • German bond yields surge higher after deal to loosen debt brake

Bank of America revised up their forecasts for the euro:

  • now see EUR/USD reaching $1.15 by the end of 2025
  • and to around $1.20 by the end of 2026

data.title: Templeton Emerging Markets Investment Trust Buys Back 1 Million Ordinary Shares for Cancellation
data.description:
Templeton Emerging Markets Investment Trust purchased for cancellation 1 million ordinary shares at an average price of 1.73 pounds sterling apiece, according to a Monday filing with the New Zealand bourse.

Following the transaction, the company's share capital now consists of 1.03 billion shares in issue and over 103.8 million shares held in treasury, per the filing.

data.title: Morgan Stanley impressed with Superloop after earnings season; backs board execution
data.description:
** Analysts at Morgan Stanley positive on Australia's Superloop after February earnings season

** Brokerage sees an attractive risk reward situation for the telecommunications company, with earnings upside expectations from improved performance at consumer and wholesale segments

** Sees company having an extended runway for growth as it leverages its low-cost model to take market share from current incumbents

** Adds Superloop leadership team has executed very strongly over the last year

** Brokerage has A$2.65 PT on stock and rates it "overweight"

** Says contract win from Origin Energy is positive for co

** Stock down 4.6% YTD

data.title: OCBC Transfers 196,713 Treasury Shares for Employee Schemes
data.description:
Oversea-Chinese Banking transferred 196,713 treasury shares for employee share schemes on March 7, valued at SG$2.8 million, according to a Friday filing on the Singapore Exchange.

This reduces OCBC's treasury shares to 18.2 million, or 0.4052% of outstanding shares, from 18.4 million, or 0.4096%, before the transfer.

data.title: Tyro Payments Target Price Cut 41% to A$0.82/Share by Macquarie>TYR.AU
Tyro Payments Cut to Neutral From Outperform by Macquarie >TYR.AU
Orica Target Price Raised 0.6% to A$20.91/Share by Macquarie>ORI.AU
